rationale: In the citing paper, Ascarza et al. (2012) is cited only in passing as background support for a two-part tariff context and for the general idea that subscription fees affect retention. The citation appears in grouped references and is not used to develop the paper's core hypotheses about free-trial acquisition, nor to motivate its main identification strategy or model structure. The citing study does not engage the cited paper's specific contribution on three-part tariffs, free-unit valuation, or learning. This indicates a contextual mention rather than a substantive dependency.
